title: Chapter Thirty-One: The Guanzhong Plain
content:
## JEONG YEON-SHIN

Cheers rose from behind him, rolling across the Guanzhong Plain like distant thunder.

Even the Nine Sects masters, who held themselves above such things, joined the roar. Young martial artists who had seen Yeon-shin shield Jeong Hye. Others who had shared drink and sparring promises on Zhongnan Mountain. Their voices blended into one sound, fierce and bright.

"Lightning Flash. A fitting name."

"Radiant Wing Sword Style -- just as we heard."

"For Wei Wu-hyeok, who was the Tyrant Sword Sect's rising star, to fall in two exchanges..."

The Tyrant Sword Sect's camp was different. A murmur spread through their ranks -- not loud, but visible in their faces. Bewilderment. Disbelief.

The sect leader's disciple had lost his head in two moves. To a boy his own age.

"Kill them all."

The Tyrant Sword Sect Leader's voice cut across the plain, low and resonant, laced with enough true energy to press against the chest like a physical weight. Morale was a fragile thing, and he would not let his crack.

"Yeon-shin! Come back!"

Hyeon Won-chang. Shouting his name like a friend rather than a junior. How urgent it must have been.

But Yeon-shin's eyes had already found the Tyrant Sword Sect Leader. The man stood motionless, his sword planted in the earth, his face unchanged by his disciple's death. Yeon-shin formed his lips into a wide, deliberate line.

*I will take one of your arms. Somehow.*

Absurd. He knew it. The sect leader would have taken it as entertainment from a child.

A faint smile appeared on the man's face. He snickered.

Then the Tyrant Sword Sect charged.

A vibration like a war drum rolled through their camp. Banners snapped taut. Hundreds of feet pounded the dry earth, and the Zhongnan Sect rose to meet them, pouring down the slope like stormwater to shield their vanguard.

The roar of it swallowed the plain.

*So this is a group battle of masters.*

Yeon-shin retreated, eyes sweeping the Tyrant Sword Sect's line. They came fast -- lightness techniques folding the distance, voices carrying true energy that bounced between the low hills like echoes in a gorge.

At the front charged a man with a thundering stride and an elegant beard whipping in the wind. The energy wave pouring from him cracked the dry earth beneath his feet.

Elder-level. One glance was enough.

"I'll kill you!" the old man roared. Wei Wu-hyeok's grandfather, by the fury in his eyes.

"If you had taught him well," Yeon-shin said, "it would not have been just two exchanges."

"How dare you!"

The provocation worked. Killing intent flooded from the elder like heat from a forge -- blinding, narrowing. Exactly what Yeon-shin wanted.

*This is a battlefield. Any method is permitted.*

He turned sideways, creating space, and the Zhongnan Sword Immortal flew past, close enough to brush his sleeve.

"Thanks to you, we've taken the initiative."

A sound like a mountain splitting. The Sword Immortal's palm strike sent the elder hurtling backward, energy scattering in all directions.

The sect war had begun.

---

Yeon-shin moved through the melee like a leaf on a river of blades.

The Jeong Family Dynamic Technique filled his body, and the energy wave that rose from him was heavier than it had been even a week ago. His clothes -- no Desolate Sect uniform today, only guest robes -- fluttered as he stamped the ground and surged toward a cluster of young Tyrant Sword fighters rushing in with eyes full of rage.

The first one used a spear. True energy coiled along the shaft, spiralling, carrying real killing intent.

He cut through it.

The Desolate Sword sheared the rotating energy wave, and in the instant confusion filled the spearman's eyes, the Radiant Wing Sword Style's dim light passed his neck.

*Two.*

He did not treat them as people. On a battlefield, achievements were numbers. Ethics and compassion were doors you closed and locked.

White sword light flashed in all directions. The upper dantian blazed at his crown, throwing formless awareness across every angle. Even when strikes came from behind, he deflected and cut without turning his head.

"Coordinate! Don't rush in recklessly!"

"Watch his legs -- even his footwork is impossible to read!"

They learned. Came at him in groups, covering each other's angles. But the harmony between his Rotational Strike and the Radiant Wing Sword Style was seamless now -- a spiralling wall of light that slid between every gap in their coordination.

Hyeon Won-chang guarded his back. And one by one, the Desolate Sword drank.

It did not stop until the first battle reached its lull.

---

Zhongnan won.

The aftermath was a fever of celebration. They said the initiative Yeon-shin had seized at the start had made all the difference. He walked the mountain paths and could not go ten paces without someone calling his name.

"I have fruit wine hidden from my master -- would you care for some?"

"The sparring list is already full? I came too late."

"The sisters at Poetry Rhythm Pavilion seemed interested, but I cut them off. Sword discussion first, yes?"

He recalled his conversation with Yeo Il-sin. She had offered to tell him the reason for the Jeong family's annihilation. Said his second brother already knew.

*There probably will not be much shock.*

He told himself that. It was an unnecessary worry. Though he ran from death with every breath, people insisted on treating him like a child in need of shelter.

"Just in case," he murmured.

He would not carry anything that could fracture his focus during battle. Inner demons come unexpectedly. He would hear the truth when he left Zhongnan Mountain.

"Sir Jeong!"

Hyeon Won-chang waved from a courtyard. Yeon-shin approached with a thin smile.

"Two renowned masters from Xi'an have come. They asked if you would train with them."

"Precipice Sword and Shadowless Fist?"

"Your reputation precedes you. The Sword Immortal treats you like his own grandson, and even the Mount Hua Sword Saint discussed making you a guest --"

Time moved pleasantly. White clouds drifted in the sunlight.

Three days later, the Tyrant Sword Sect returned to the Guanzhong Plain with sharpened swords.

---

The second battle was different.

Looking across the field, Yeon-shin could feel the change in the enemy camp. Faces hidden. Elder Council members who had stayed behind the first time now stood at the front, energy waves rolling off them like heat from sun-baked stone.

"This will not be easy," the Zhongnan Sword Immortal said gravely. "All the old men have come out."

"If we win here," Ou Yang Chen said, "it ends."

Wei Ji Myo-hwa's eyes met Yeon-shin's across the command group. Her complexion was different from before -- sharper, brighter, as if she had shed some old weight. Power surged through her.

"I wish you martial fortune, Sir Jeong."

"I hope the Sword Dragon stays safe."

The Zhongnan Sword Immortal touched his shoulder. "Beware the Eight Fierce Rakshasa Sword. He is the grandfather of the man you killed. I had to face the Elder Council Leader before I could finish him. His stance crumbled quickly, but even while receiving my blade, he was watching you."

"His rank is above the sect leader's," Wei Ji Myo-hwa added. "They call him the right arm."

"Thank you."

No vanguard match this time. The Tyrant Sword Sect came with lightness techniques at full speed, a wall of killing intent that made the earth shudder.

The collision was instantaneous.

Energy waves swept from all directions. Swords rang. Bodies fell. The Poetry Rhythm Pavilion disciples formed a sword formation on the left flank, and the battle swallowed everything.

Yeon-shin found himself raising the Radiant Wing Sword Style in the thick of it, the smell of blood already familiar, his count already climbing.

Then he came.

An old man in tattered clothes, battle-worn, tremendous momentum pouring off him despite his ragged state. The Eight Fierce Rakshasa Sword. His eyes were fire.

"No need for long words!"

"Is that how all evil sects are?" Yeon-shin said. "You know no shame."

"I'll teach you a lesson!"

*He is enraged. Easy to analyze.*

The elder's strikes were enormous. The ground cracked with each swing -- not from blade contact but from the sheer pressure of his sword force. Yeon-shin deployed Rotating Strike, slipped past a descending blow, and brushed against the old man's side.

"You --"

A palm caught him in the ribs.

The impact exploded through his oblique muscles and launched him backward through the air. For a heartbeat, his vision went white.

*Jeong Family Dynamic Technique!*

The middle dantian responded. True energy broke through the impact, dissipating the force wave before it could spread, and by the time his feet touched earth again he was whole.

Wei Ji Myo-hwa, Shadowless Fist, and Precipice Sword descended on the elder simultaneously. Four high-level masters exchanging blows, energy scattering in arcs of white and crimson.

And Yeon-shin watched.

His upper dantian blazed. Every technique the Eight Fierce Rakshasa Sword used was being engraved in his mind -- grip, muscle movement, footwork habits, the angle of his wrist when raising foot force. He dissected it all.

At fifty moves, the elder used a technique of mutual harm. He sacrificed his left arm to Wei Ji Myo-hwa's sword to drive a strike into her abdomen. Shadowless Fist and Precipice Sword fell too, hit by his right-hand blade.

The old man was in tatters. One arm gone. Blood at his mouth. Internal injuries severe.

But he was still standing.

Hyeon Won-chang rushed in. Got hurled aside like a training dummy.

The elder advanced on him, sword raised. One more heartbeat and Hyeon Won-chang's head would leave his shoulders.

Yeon-shin was already there.

Standing at the old man's flank. Close enough to see the calluses on his remaining hand.

"I've been waiting!" the elder snarled.

His sword stance changed -- a tremendous swift technique erupting from his wrinkled hand, the diagonal strike blazing upward toward Yeon-shin's chest.

*I can feel it.*

Not a sword he could see. Not with his eye technique, not at this speed. This was something else.

Foresight.

His crown burned white-hot. The upper dantian erupted with a searing light that felt like the sun plunging into his skull. Every technique he had catalogued, every habit, every micro-pattern -- all of it converging on this single instant.

The Desolate Sword extended like flowing water.

Destruction.

The light cluster of falling sunlight distorted along the blade. The white trajectory slipped past the Rakshasa's sword like a stream finding the path of least resistance and passed the old man's neck.

Blood.

In the battlefield where all manner of energy and wind swirled, only where Yeon-shin stood was quiet.

He grabbed the corpse by the hair. Lifted the head high.

His gaze found the Tyrant Sword Sect Leader across the chaos. The man had looked this way -- had seen it happen. For the first time, bewilderment showed on that iron face.

"Tyrant Sword Sect Leader." Yeon-shin's voice carried true energy, resonating across the plain. Masters of both sects turned. Swords stilled. "I said I would take it."

He held the head higher.

"Is this your arm too?"


---
